title: Co to je? Identifying objects
summary: Ask what an object is, identify it, and check a guess with yes/no forms.
objectives: Check a guess with Je to…? and correct it with není

sections.heading: Ask and identify
sections.body: Co to je? asks what an object is. The answer normally begins To je…
examples: Co to je? — To je kočka.
Co to je? — To je město.
Co to je? — To je telefon.

sections.heading: Check a guess
sections.body: Invert the opening words to ask whether your guess is correct. Není is the negative form of je.
examples: Je to batoh? — Ano, to je batoh.
Je to kočka? — Ne, to není kočka. To je pes.

sections.heading: Something close by
sections.body: Tohle points to a thing close to the speaker. Co je tohle? means “What is this right here?”
examples: Co je tohle? — Tohle je tužka.

resources.title: Original lesson: Co to je?
resources.note: Original identification patterns and illustrated examples.

exercises.explanation: The core question is Co to je?

exercises.prompt: Which answer says “No, this is not a cat”?
exercises.explanation: Není is the negative form of je.
To je telefon means “This/that is a telephone.”
